title: cuscuta epilinum var. halophyta
intro: Cuscuta epilinum var. halophyta, also known as Halophyte Dodder or Chinese Dodder, is a type of parasitic plant that belongs to the Cuscuta genus. It has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain and swelling, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues and skin conditions. In traditional Chinese medicine, Cuscuta epilinum var. halophyta is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tonics, although its modern use and availability may vary.
